name: hipaa
description: HIPAA-specific rules for code and security reviews handling Protected Health Information (PHI). Layer 2 of the four-layer rubric composition (base + regimes + domain + project).

severity_rules.pattern: Logger or print statement emits PHI fields (medical record number, diagnosis, treatment, insurance id, DOB, full name with health context) in plaintext.
severity_rules.description: Logging PHI in plaintext is a HIPAA breach (45 CFR 164.312) and triggers breach-notification obligations.
severity_rules.remediation: Strip or hash PHI at the logger boundary; route audit logs through a HIPAA-compliant log store with access controls.

severity_rules.pattern: URL path or query string contains PHI identifiers (medical-record id, patient id, diagnosis codes).
severity_rules.description: PHI in URLs leaks into access logs, browser history, and referrers — a Privacy Rule violation under 45 CFR 164.502.
severity_rules.remediation: Move PHI to the request body or use opaque correlation ids that do not reveal patient identity.

severity_rules.pattern: Error response body or stack trace echoes PHI fields back to the caller.
severity_rules.description: PHI in error messages can be captured by client-side telemetry and constitutes an unauthorised disclosure.
severity_rules.remediation: Replace PHI in error responses with opaque correlation ids; log full context server-side only.

severity_rules.pattern: PHI read/write code path with no audit-log emission identifying actor, timestamp, and record id.
severity_rules.description: 45 CFR 164.312(b) requires audit controls — every PHI access must be recorded.
severity_rules.remediation: Emit a tamper-evident audit-log entry with (actor_id, action, record_id, timestamp, reason_code) on every PHI touch.

severity_rules.pattern: Query selects whole patient record (`SELECT * FROM patients`) or returns PHI fields not required by the consuming endpoint.
severity_rules.description: The minimum-necessary standard (45 CFR 164.502(b)) limits PHI disclosure to what is required for the purpose.
severity_rules.remediation: Project only the fields the caller needs; encode PHI minimisation into the persistence and API layers.

severity_rules.pattern: PHI persisted in a database, blob store, or message bus column without column-level or transparent encryption-at-rest configured.
severity_rules.description: Unencrypted PHI at rest fails the encryption safe harbour (45 CFR 164.312(a)(2)(iv)) and exposes the project to breach-notification.
severity_rules.remediation: Enable AES-256 column or TDE encryption; document key custody and rotation procedure.

severity_rules.pattern: PHI transmitted via HTTP, plain TCP, or FTP — no TLS 1.2+ enforcement on the transport.
severity_rules.description: PHI in transit must be encrypted with FIPS 140-2 cryptography. Unencrypted transmission is a per-record breach.
severity_rules.remediation: Enforce HTTPS/TLS 1.2+ on the transport; reject plaintext requests at the edge.

severity_rules.pattern: Outbound call to a third-party processor (analytics, email, SMS, AI/LLM) sends PHI without a documented Business Associate Agreement reference.
severity_rules.description: 45 CFR 164.314(a) requires a signed BAA before sharing PHI with any business associate.
severity_rules.remediation: Document the BAA reference (vendor name, contract id, expiry) at the integration boundary; deny PHI to vendors without a BAA.

severity_rules.pattern: Project has no breach-detection event hook or notification handler that fires on suspected PHI disclosure.
severity_rules.description: 45 CFR 164.404 requires breach notification within 60 days. Without a detection hook the timer cannot be respected.
severity_rules.remediation: Wire a breach-detection event into security monitoring; route to a documented notification runbook.
